title: Thursday's Three Things
description: The /θ/ sound (THIN, THREE, THANK). Why Brazilians say 'tree' for 'three' — and how to fix it with your tongue between your teeth.
summary_pt_br: O som /θ/ (th mudo) em THIN, THREE, THANK. A dica da língua entre os dentes, treinada com a música Three Little Birds do Bob Marley e Thinking Out Loud do Ed Sheeran.

scenes.subtitle_en: The sound that Brazilians love to hate
scenes.subtitle_pt: O som que os brasileiros adoram odiar

scenes.text_en:
Bia said 'TREE birds in a TREE!'
Mr. Kumar laughed: 'Not TREE — say THREE.
Touch your teeth with your tongue, very light.
Blow a tiny bit of air. Don't bite!'
scenes.text_pt:
A Bia falou: 'TREE passarinhos numa TREE!'
Mr. Kumar riu: 'Não é TREE — é THREE.
Encoste a ponta da língua nos dentes, bem leve.
Sopre um pouquinho de ar. Sem morder de greve!'

scenes.title: How to make the TH sound (the voiceless one)
scenes.body_en: Stick the tip of your tongue LIGHTLY between your top and bottom teeth. Now blow air through — no voice, just air. You should feel cold air on your tongue. That's /θ/ — the sound in THIN, THREE, THANK, THURSDAY.
scenes.body_pt: Coloque a pontinha da língua entre os dentes de cima e os de baixo — só a ponta, de leve. Agora sopre ar (sem voz, sem 't', sem 'f'). Você vai sentir o ar frio passando pela língua. Esse é o /θ/ de THIN, THREE, THANK, THURSDAY.

scenes.text_en:
Bob Marley sang it, soft and free:
'Don't worry 'bout a THING,
'cause every little THING gonna be all right.'
Three little birds. THREE. Not TREE.
scenes.text_pt:
Bob Marley cantou com leveza e fé:
'Don't worry 'bout a THING (não se preocupe com NADA),
'cause every little THING gonna be all right (cada coisinha vai dar certo).'
Three little birds. THREE. Não TREE.

exercise.question: 'I ___ you for the taco.' (agradecer)
exercise.explanation: THANK /θæŋk/ with the voiceless TH — tongue between the teeth, just air. Not 'tank' (military vehicle!) and not 'sank' (past of sink).
exercise.hint_pt_br: THANK começa com o som /θ/ — língua entre os dentes, sopro de ar.

scenes.location_en: Ed Sheeran playing on Rafa's Bluetooth speaker
scenes.location_pt: Ed Sheeran tocando na caixa do Rafa

turns.en: Hey Bia, what's Ed Sheeran thinking out loud?
turns.pt: Oi Bia, o que o Ed Sheeran tá pensando em voz alta?

turns.en: He's THINKING about love. Not TINKING. THINKING.
turns.pt: Ele tá PENSANDO no amor. Não 'tinkando'. THINKING.

turns.en: I'm THINKING about tacos. Three tacos, thank you.
turns.pt: Eu tô PENSANDO em tacos. Três tacos, por favor.

exercise.question: Complete the song line: 'Don't worry 'bout a ___.' (Bob Marley — Three Little Birds)
exercise.explanation: The chorus repeats 'thing' eleven times. Practice it — you hear perfect /θ/ with a soft voice.
exercise.hint_pt_br: THING com o /θ/. Bob Marley repete umas onze vezes — ótimo treino.

exercise.question: Which of these DOES NOT have the /θ/ sound?
exercise.explanation: THIS has /ð/ — the VOICED TH (with vocal vibration). Think/thing/thank all have /θ/ (voiceless). We'll learn /ð/ next lesson.
exercise.hint_pt_br: THIS é /ð/ — TH VIBRADO (com voz). Os outros três são /θ/ (só ar). Próxima aula detalha isso.

exercise.explanation: Four words, all starting with /θ/. Stack them: 'Three thousand people on Thursday.' Practice it aloud.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Quatro palavrinhas com /θ/. Pratique a frase 'Three thousand people on Thursday' em voz alta.

scenes.text_en:
Cosmo tried to bark a TH.
He blew. He barked. He did his best.
Woof /θ/ woof — the dog said it true.
'Thanks, Cosmo,' Mr. Kumar said. 'That'll do.'
scenes.text_pt:
Cosmo tentou latir um TH.
Soprou. Latiu. Fez o possível, puxa!
'Woof /θ/ woof' — o cão acertou, que coisa.
'Thanks, Cosmo,' disse Mr. Kumar. 'Já serviu.'

scenes.title: The Thursday ritual
scenes.pt_summary: TH + números — three things every Thursday.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Every Thursday I do three things.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: First, I call my mother in India.
turns.pt: Primeiro, ligo pra minha mãe na Índia.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Second, I eat Thai food for dinner.
turns.pt: Segundo, como comida tailandesa no jantar.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Third, I thank three people for something.
turns.pt: Terceiro, agradeço três pessoas por algo.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I will try three things this Thursday.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.pt: Maravilha. Me conta semana que vem.

scenes.title: Listening story — Thursday's Three Things
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Mr. Kumar and a student. The /θ/ sound (THIN, THREE, THANK). Why Brazilians say 'tree' for 'three' — and how to fix it with your tongue between your teeth. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Kumar
paragraphs.en: Every Thursday I do three things.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Kumar
paragraphs.en: First, I call my mother in India.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Kumar
paragraphs.en: Second, I eat Thai food for dinner.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Mr. Kumar. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Thursday's Three Things". The /θ/ sound (THIN, THREE, THANK). Why Brazilians say 'tree' for 'three' — and how to fix it with your tongue between your teeth. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Thursday's Three Things". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Thursday's Three Things'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Thursday's Three Things".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
